title: ZFlow Eval Scenarios
description: Test scenarios for verifying ZFlow multi-agent workflow system works correctly. These are template scenarios — actual eval implementation would use a more sophisticated runner with mock agents and output capture.

scenarios.name: Simple Feature (No UI)
scenarios.description: A straightforward backend feature with no UI work. Tests the minimal happy path through the dev workflow without Phase 3.5.

trigger.user_message: I want to add a rate limiter middleware to my Express API. It should limit requests to 100 per minute per IP address and return 429 when exceeded.

assertions: ui_work flag should be false
scope should mention rate limiting
scope should mention Express middleware
should identify existing middleware patterns
should map Express version and related packages
should propose at least 2 approaches
task breakdown should have success criteria per task
should include reviewer findings appendix
solution should not have drifted from scope
all tasks from solution should be completed
every changed file should trace to scope
should have zero blocker or critical issues
security audit should check for rate limit bypass
CHANGELOG should be updated
commit message should follow conventional commits

scenarios.name: Complex Feature with UI (Pencil.dev)
scenarios.description: A feature involving both backend and frontend UI work. Tests the full dev workflow including Phase 3.5 UI design with Pencil.dev.

trigger.user_message: Build a notification center for my dashboard app. It should have a bell icon with unread count, a dropdown panel showing recent notifications, and mark-as-read functionality. Notifications come from a WebSocket connection.

assertions: ui_work flag should be true
scope should mention notification center, dropdown, WebSocket
should propose multiple scope tiers (minimal/standard/full)
ui-system-scout agent should be included in the swarm
should survey existing component library and design tokens
should identify WebSocket infrastructure
should include .pen file with visual designs
design tokens should match existing system
accessibility requirements should be documented
ui-implementer agent should be used for UI tasks
implementation should reference ui-design-report.md
ui-visual-qa agent should compare against Pencil exports
should check responsive behavior at all breakpoints

scenarios.name: Debug Workflow (Root Cause Fix)
scenarios.description: A bug report triggers the debug workflow. Tests all 6 debug phases from reproduction through verification.

trigger.user_message: Users are getting 500 errors when they submit the checkout form with international addresses. The error only happens with non-ASCII characters in the address field.

assertions: reproduction steps should use non-ASCII characters
should capture the actual error output/stack trace
should trace the encoding issue through the call chain
security-impact-assessor should check for injection via encoding
history analysis should check recent changes to address handling
should distinguish symptom (500 error) from root cause (encoding)
confidence level should be stated
blast radius should be assessed
fix should be minimal and address root cause
regression risk should be documented
Karpathy: should be smallest effective change
every changed line should trace to the fix design
no speculative changes beyond the fix scope
original reproduction steps should now pass
regression tests should all pass
security verifier should confirm no new vulnerabilities

scenarios.name: Phase Skipping (Quick Fix)
scenarios.description: A user provides a well-defined task that should skip brainstorm and research phases, jumping directly to design.

trigger.user_message: Skip to design. I need to add a `created_at` timestamp field to the User model. The DB is PostgreSQL using Prisma. Just design the migration and implement it.

assertions: should reference the specific model and field
should include database migration plan
should address backward compatibility
scope.md should NOT be generated
research-report.md should NOT be generated

scenarios.name: Resume From Interruption
scenarios.description: Simulates a workflow that was interrupted after Phase 4 (implement) and needs to resume at Phase 5 (QA). Tests that the workspace state is correctly preserved and the orchestrator can pick up where it left off.

trigger.user_message: Resume my ZFlow workflow. I was in the middle of implementing the notification center feature.

pre_conditions.description: Workspace must have completed phases 00-04 before this eval runs

assertions: should read impl-report.md as input
should validate all implemented tasks from the solution
should read the full document chain for context
should NOT re-run brainstorm through implement phases
should detect existing phase outputs and skip them
current-phase.json should update to reflect resumed progress

scenarios.name: QA Blocker Loop-Back
scenarios.description: Tests the scenario where QA finds blocker issues that require looping back to the implement phase for targeted fixes.

trigger.user_message: I just finished implementing the auth module. Run QA on it.

pre_conditions.description: Workspace has completed phases through implementation

assertions: first QA pass should find blocker issues
second QA pass should have zero blockers
security audit should be included
second impl report should only address QA findings
changes should be targeted — not a full re-implementation
should loop back to implement after blocker QA findings
second QA pass should confirm blockers are resolved
should NOT loop back a third time if second QA passes

features_not_tested: escalation pattern (3 failed fix attempts in debug)

metadata.notes: This is a template eval framework. A production implementation would include: (1) a test runner that sets up mock workspaces, (2) mock agent responses for deterministic testing, (3) output capture and assertion evaluation, (4) timeout handling for stuck phases, and (5) cleanup between scenarios.
